articles.title: U.S. and Iran Head to Pakistan: A New Chance for Peace?
articles.summary: After two months of war, the U.S. and Iran are sending diplomats to Pakistan for talks. The big sticking point is the Strait of Hormuz, a crucial waterway for oil shipments that's mostly blocked. The White House confirmed that special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Islamabad for direct talks. Iran's foreign minister is already there, but says no meeting is planned yet. A ceasefire has held for three weeks. The U.S. also announced new sanctions on Iran and Chinese companies accused of transporting Iranian oil. Will diplomacy finally work?

articles.title: More Than a Merry-Go-Round: Smithsonian Carousel Reopens with a Civil Rights Story
articles.summary: After nearly three years of restoration, the Smithsonian National Carousel on the National Mall is spinning again. But this carousel has a powerful history. Originally located at Gwynn Oak Amusement Park in Maryland, it was segregated until August 28, 1963 — the same day Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his "I Have a Dream" speech. That day, 11-month-old Sharon Langley became the first Black child to ride. Now 63, she rode again on a horse named "Freedom Rider." The carousel features 54 horses, a sea monster, a pig, and two chariots. Kids love it — one 7-year-old called it "one of the fastest carousels" he's been on.

articles.title: Alcaraz's French Open Threepeat Hopes Crushed by Wrist Injury
articles.summary: Reigning two-time French Open champion Carlos Alcaraz has withdrawn from this year's tournament due to a right wrist injury. The 22-year-old Spaniard also pulled out of the Italian Open, ending his clay season. Alcaraz sustained the injury during the Barcelona Open and tried to play through it, but tests revealed the need for caution. He became the youngest man to complete the career Grand Slam in January by winning the Australian Open. His rival Jannik Sinner, who took the world No. 1 spot from Alcaraz, called it "sad news" and hopes Alcaraz returns for Wimbledon. The French Open runs from May 24 to June 7.
